articles.title: LG Energy Solution secures $1.8 bil. ESS deal with DTE Energy
articles.summary: LG Energy Solution's U.S. subsidiary, LG Energy Solution Vertech, has finalized a $1.8 billion supply agreement with DTE Energy to deploy Michigan-made battery energy storage systems across the U.S. state. This deal covers 6 gigawatt-hours (GWh) of storage capacity, with the systems providing 1.5 gigawatts of battery storage over the next two years. The partnership aims to enhance grid reliability, support clean energy deployment, and utilize battery cells produced at LG Energy Solution facilities in Michigan and other North American sites.
articles.content: LG Energy Solution's U.S. subsidiary, LG Energy Solution Vertech, has finalized a $1.8 billion supply agreement with DTE Energy to deploy Michigan-made battery energy storage systems across the U.S. state. This deal covers 6 gigawatt-hours (GWh) of storage capacity, with the systems providing 1.5 gigawatts of battery storage over the next two years. The partnership aims to enhance grid reliability, support clean energy deployment, and utilize battery cells produced at LG Energy Solution facilities in Michigan and other North American sites.

articles.title: Oppenheimer upgrades Quanta Services stock rating on infrastructure growth
articles.summary: Oppenheimer has upgraded Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) stock from Perform to Outperform, setting an $800 price target due to the company's strong position in high-growth infrastructure markets like electrical transmission, power generation, and network modernization. Despite appearing overvalued by some metrics, analysts have increased earnings estimates, and the company recently reported strong Q1 2026 results, a new dividend, and a $1 billion stock repurchase program. These factors, combined with secular drivers like electrification and AI development, are expected to sustain Quanta's valuation
articles.content: Oppenheimer has upgraded Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) stock from Perform to Outperform, setting an $800 price target due to the company's strong position in high-growth infrastructure markets like electrical transmission, power generation, and network modernization. Despite appearing overvalued by some metrics, analysts have increased earnings estimates, and the company recently reported strong Q1 2026 results, a new dividend, and a $1 billion stock repurchase program. These factors, combined with secular drivers like electrification and AI development, are expected to sustain Quanta's valuation premium and potential for margin expansion.

articles.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights First Solar, Enphase Energy and Canadian Solar
articles.summary: The U.S. solar market is experiencing strong demand due to rising electricity consumption, AI-powered data centers, and corporate clean-energy targets, with solar capacity projected to nearly triple by 2036. However, policy uncertainty, tighter tax-credit timelines, supply-chain pressures, and changes to residential solar economics are creating ongoing challenges. This Zacks Industry Outlook highlights First Solar, Enphase Energy, and Canadian Solar as key companies for investors to monitor despite current industry headwinds.
articles.content: The U.S. solar market is experiencing strong demand due to rising electricity consumption, AI-powered data centers, and corporate clean-energy targets, with solar capacity projected to nearly triple by 2036. However, policy uncertainty, tighter tax-credit timelines, supply-chain pressures, and changes to residential solar economics are creating ongoing challenges. This Zacks Industry Outlook highlights First Solar, Enphase Energy, and Canadian Solar as key companies for investors to monitor despite current industry headwinds.
